html:
“Certain items can be put on the rack and begin drying. After drying for some time, the item will turn into a dried version of itself.”

-

The Drying Rack is a craftable Structure found in the Cooking Filter or Structures Filter. It requires 3 Twigs, 2 Charcoal, and 3 Rope to craft and a Science Machine to prototype.

The Drying Rack has three slots to dry most Meats, Kelp Fronds or Petals into their dried form. Dried food items take significantly longer to spoil and often provide better Health and Sanity compared to raw food used in the rack or their cooked versions.

Unlike the results of Cooking, the dried food created will always be completely fresh. This means a spoiled Morsel can be dried to get a completely fresh piece of Small Jerky. Dried food left on the Drying Rack will spoil over time. Rain will prevent any items on the Drying Rack from drying, making it less effective in Spring.

When a Drying Rack is burned down, any items hanging on it will immediately drop in their dried form.

Dryable Items

The list of possible Food that can be dried:

Item Dried Time required Spoil Timer
2 days 20 days
1 day 20 days
1 day 15 days

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Woven - Elegant Butcher's Rack skin was introduced during the official Year of the Carrat event in 2020.
  • The Woven - Elegant Victorian Meat Rack skin was introduced during the official Year of the Beefalo event in 2021.
Woven - Elegant
Yeara of the Carrat Collection
Butcher's Rack
A very normal piece of food prep equipment that isn't disconcerting at all.
See ingame
Woven - Elegant
Musclebound Collection
Hanging Bar
An exercise bar is the perfect thing for toughening up your food.
Woven - Elegant
Victorian Collection
Victorian Meat Rack
A distinguished display for fine dried meats.
Woven - Elegant
Ancient Collection
Archaic Meat Rack
Archaeologists speculate that the meat found on this drying rack was way past the expiration date.

Tips

  • Drying Racks are very useful for storing Meats as Jerky and Small Jerky take 20 days to completely spoil, while Meat and Morsel will spoil in 6 days, and cooked Meat/Morsel will spoil in 10 days. Storing any kind of jerky in the Ice Box will give 40 days before they spoil, allowing the player to focus on concerns other than gathering fresh food.
  • It's not necessary to dry Frog Legs or Drumsticks and turn them into Jerky or Small Jerky because those ingredients can be used to make Crock Pot meals that are more health efficient, such as a Froggle Bunwich.
  • Rain will stop the drying process and the food can possibly spoil if it rains for too long. An activated Umbralla on the ground can protect the drying rack from the rain.
  • Dried leafy materials can be used to trade teas at Pearl's Tea Shop.

Trivia

  • Even though the Koalefant Trunk counts as meat, it cannot be hung on the Drying Rack.
  • The Hermit Drying Rack was introduced to Don't Starve Together in the She Sells Sea Shells update.
  • The Drying Rack used to only have one slot before getting two additional slots in the Hostile Takeover: Phase Two update to work similar to Woby's Drying Rack.
  • As of the Winter's Feast 2025 update, the Drying Rack can dry fish meat into Fishy Jerky, and also dry petals and other similar leafy materials.

Tips[edit | edit source]
Drying Racks are very useful for storing Meats as Jerky and Small Jerky take 20 days to completely spoil, while Meat and Morsel will spoil in 6 days, and cooked Meat/Morsel will spoil in 10 days. Storing any kind of jerky in the Ice Box will give 40 days before they spoil, allowing the player to focus on concerns other than gathering fresh food.
It's not necessary to dry Frog Legs or Drumsticks and turn them into Jerky or Small Jerky because those ingredients can be used to make Crock Pot meals that are more health efficient, such as a Froggle Bunwich.
Rain will stop the drying process and the food can possibly spoil if it rains for too long. An activated Umbralla on the ground can protect the drying rack from the rain.
Dried leafy materials can be used to trade teas at Pearl's Tea Shop.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Even though the Koalefant Trunk counts as meat, it cannot be hung on the Drying Rack.
The Hermit Drying Rack was introduced to Don't Starve Together in the She Sells Sea Shells update.
The Drying Rack used to only have one slot before getting two additional slots in the Hostile Takeover: Phase Two update to work similar to Woby's Drying Rack.
As of the Winter's Feast 2025 update, the Drying Rack can dry fish meat into Fishy Jerky, and also dry petals and other similar leafy materials.

After drying for some time, the item will turn into a dried version of itself.}} The '''Drying Rack''' is a craftable [[Structure]] found in the [[Cooking Filter]] or [[Structures Filter]]. It requires 3 [[Twigs]], 2 [[Charcoal]], and 3 [[Rope]] to craft and a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. The Drying Rack has three slots to dry most [[Meats/DST|Meats]], [[Kelp Fronds]] or [[Petals/DST|Petals]] into their dried form. Dried food items take significantly longer to spoil and often provide better [[Health]] and [[Sanity]] compared to raw food used in the rack or their cooked versions. Unlike the results of [[Cooking]], the dried food created will always be completely fresh. This means a spoiled Morsel can be dried to get a completely fresh piece of Small Jerky. Dried food left on the Drying Rack will spoil over time. [[Rain]] will prevent any items on the Drying Rack from drying, making it less effective in [[Spring]]. When a Drying Rack is burned down, any items hanging on it will immediately drop in their dried form. == Dryable Items == The list of possible Food that can be dried: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" align="center" !Item !Dried !Time required !Spoil Timer |- |{{pic32|Meat}} ||{{pic32|Jerky}}||2 days |20 days |- |{{pic32|Monster Meat}}||{{pic32|Monster Jerky}}||1 day |20 days |- |{{pic32|Raw Fish}} ||{{pic32|Fishy Jerky}}||1 day |15 days |- |{{pic32|Batilisk Wing}} {{pic32|Naked Nostrils}} |rowspan=2|{{pic32|Small Jerky}}||2 days |20 days |- |{{pic32|Morsel}} {{pic32|Frog Legs}} {{pic32|Drumstick}} ||1 day |20 days |- |{{pic32|Fish Morsel}} |rowspan=2|{{pic32|Small Fishy Jerky}} ||0.5 day |15 days |- |{{pic32|Eel}} ||1 day |15 days |- |{{pic32|Petals}} ||{{pic32|Dried Petals}} ||1 day |x |- |{{pic32|Dark Petals}} ||{{pic32|Dried Dark Petals}} ||1 day |x |- |{{pic32|Foliage}} ||{{pic32|Dried Foliage}} ||1 day |x |- |{{pic32|Succulent}} ||{{pic32|Dried Succulent}} ||1 day |x |- |{{pic32|Lune Tree Blossom}} ||{{pic32|Dried Lune Tree Blossom}} ||1 day |x |- |{{pic32|Fire Nettle Fronds}} ||{{pic32|Dried Fire Nettle Frond}} ||1 day |x |- |{{pic32|Tillweeds}} ||{{pic32|Dried Tillweed}} ||1 day |x |- |{{pic32|Forget-Me-Lots}} ||{{pic32|Dried Forget-Me-Lots}} ||1 day |x |- |{{pic32|Kelp Fronds}}||{{pic32|Dried Kelp Fronds}}||0.25 days |20 days |} == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Gardening and Food Skins|Belongings}} * The '''Woven - Elegant Butcher's Rack''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Carrat]] event in 2020. * The '''Woven - Elegant Victorian Meat Rack''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Beefalo]] event in 2021.
{{SkinInfo |image = Butcher's Rack Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Yeara of the Carrat Collection |name = Butcher's Rack |description = A very normal piece of food prep equipment that isn't disconcerting at all. |ingame = Butcher's Rack.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Hanging Bar Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Musclebound Collection |name = Hanging Bar |description = An exercise bar is the perfect thing for toughening up your food. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Victorian Meat Rack Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Victorian Collection |name = Victorian Meat Rack |description = A distinguished display for fine dried meats. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Archaic Meat Rack Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Ancient Collection |name = Archaic Meat Rack |description = Archaeologists speculate that the meat found on this drying rack was way past the expiration date. }}
== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * Drying Racks are very useful for storing Meats as Jerky and Small Jerky take 20 days to completely spoil, while Meat and Morsel will spoil in 6 days, and cooked Meat/Morsel will spoil in 10 days. Storing any kind of jerky in the [[Ice Box]] will give 40 days before they spoil, allowing the player to focus on concerns other than gathering fresh food. * It's not necessary to dry Frog Legs or Drumsticks and turn them into Jerky or Small Jerky because those ingredients can be used to make [[Crock Pot]] meals that are more health efficient, such as a [[Froggle Bunwich]]. * Rain will stop the drying process and the food can possibly spoil if it rains for too long. An activated [[Umbralla]] on the ground can protect the drying rack from the rain. * Dried leafy materials can be used to trade teas at [[Pearl's Tea Shop]]. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Even though the [[Koalefant Trunk]] counts as meat, it cannot be hung on the Drying Rack. * The Hermit Drying Rack was introduced to ''Don't Starve Together'' in the ''[[Return of Them#She Sells Sea Shells|She Sells Sea Shells]]'' update. * The Drying Rack used to only have one slot before getting two additional slots in the [[Hostile Takeover: Phase Two]] update to work similar to Woby's Drying
